 {"id":"bd-2z7a","title":"Implement dynamic model creation","description":"## Description

Create models dynamically at runtime.

## Python Behavior

```python
from sqlmodel import SQLModel, Field
from typing import Optional

def create_model(name: str, fields: dict):
    return type(name, (SQLModel,), {
        '__annotations__': fields,
        '__tablename__': name.lower(),
    })

DynamicUser = create_model('DynamicUser', {
    'id': Optional[int],
    'name': str,
})
```

## Rust Approach

Rust's static typing makes true dynamic models impossible, but we can:

### Option 1: Generic Dynamic Model
```rust
pub struct DynamicModel {
    table_name: String,
    columns: HashMap<String, ColumnDef>,
    values: HashMap<String, Value>,
}

impl DynamicModel {
    pub fn new(table_name: &str) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn add_column(&mut self, name: &str, def: ColumnDef) { ... }
    pub fn set(&mut self, name: &str, value: Value) { ... }
    pub fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&Value> { ... }
}
```

### Option 2: Schema from database
```rust
let schema = introspect_table(cx, conn, "users").await?;
let dynamic = DynamicModel::from_schema(schema);
```

## Use Cases

- Database introspection tools
- Generic admin interfaces
- Schema migration tools

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] DynamicModel type
- [ ] Runtime column definition
- [ ] Query execution with dynamic models
- [ ] Serialization to/from JSON
- [ ] Table creation from dynamic model","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:14:09.858162337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:14:09.874422279Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2z7a","depends_on_id":"bd-170","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zf","title":"Implement IndeterminateSpinner for unknown-length operations","description":"## Purpose
Create a spinner renderable for operations where we do not know the total count or duration, showing activity with elapsed time.

## Background
Many database operations have unknown duration:
- Establishing connections
- Running complex queries
- Waiting for locks
- Initial data discovery

Users need feedback that something is happening, even without a completion percentage.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/spinner.rs

### Core Struct
IndeterminateSpinner holds:
- message: String
- started_at: Instant
- style: SpinnerStyle enum
- theme: Theme

### SpinnerStyle Enum
- Dots: ... (three dots cycling)
- Braille: Unicode braille pattern animation
- Line: -/|\ rotating
- Arrow: arrow rotating
- Simple: asterisk blinking

### Frame Generation
Each style defines frames array and interval_ms.
Example Dots: [".", "..", "...", ".."] at 250ms intervals

### Rendering (Rich Mode)
[...] Connecting to database (2.3s)
^---- spinner animation
     ^---- message
                           ^---- elapsed time

### Plain Text (Agent Mode)
[...] Connecting to database (2.3s)
(Static output, no animation - agents see single line)

## API Design
IndeterminateSpinner::new(message)
  .style(SpinnerStyle::Dots)
  .theme(theme)
  .render(width) / .render_plain()

### Convert to Progress
When length becomes known, convert spinner to progress:
let progress = spinner.into_progress(total);

### Live Update Pattern
let spinner = IndeterminateSpinner::new("Connecting");
console.start_spinner(&spinner);
// ... do work
console.stop_spinner(&spinner);

## Verification Steps
1. Test each spinner style
2. Verify elapsed time formatting (s, m:ss)
3. Test conversion to progress bar
4. Verify plain text output
5. Test with long messages (truncation)
6. Verify animation timing

## Dependencies
- Theme from this crate
- std::time::Instant for timing","acceptance_criteria":"IndeterminateSpinner shows activity animation
Spinner has multiple style options
Spinner respects terminal width
Plain mode outputs dots or status text
All unit tests verify animation frames
Performance tests confirm low overhead","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:38.251317294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:25:34.436765300Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:25:34.436722440Z","close_reason":"Implemented IndeterminateSpinner with 5 styles, conversion to progress bar, and 20 unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zf","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:38.269504276Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zf","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:19:05.665625553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-2zf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_spinner_creation - verify initial state
2. test_spinner_all_styles - verify each SpinnerStyle
3. test_spinner_frame_generation - verify animation frames
4. test_spinner_elapsed_time - verify duration tracking
5. test_spinner_render_plain - verify text output
6. test_spinner_message_update - verify message changes
7. test_spinner_convert_to_progress - verify conversion
8. test_spinner_render_rich - verify rich output (with feature)","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-318","title":"Phase 8: Facade Crate Integration and Public API","description":"## Purpose
Integrate the console system into the main sqlmodel facade crate, providing a unified public API for console configuration and usage across all sqlmodel components.

## Background
The sqlmodel facade crate is the main entry point for users. It must:
- Re-export console types and traits
- Provide easy console configuration
- Integrate console with high-level operations
- Maintain backward compatibility for users not using console

## Key Deliverables

### 1. Console Re-exports in sqlmodel Prelude
Add to prelude:
- SqlModelConsole
- OutputMode
- Theme
- ConsoleAware trait
- Key renderables (ErrorPanel, QueryResultTable, etc.)

### 2. Console Builder Integration
Extend existing builders to accept console:
Session::builder()
    .with_console(console)
    .connect(url)

### 3. Global Console Support
Optional global console for convenience:
sqlmodel::set_global_console(console);
// Now all operations use this console automatically

### 4. Configuration API
Easy configuration without deep imports:
use sqlmodel::prelude::*;

let console = SqlModelConsole::builder()
    .auto_detect_mode()  // agent vs human
    .theme(Theme::dark())
    .build();

### 5. Feature Flag Coordination
Ensure console feature properly propagates:
- sqlmodel enables sqlmodel-console when console feature active
- Drivers enable their console support when console active
- All is optional, no bloat for non-console users

## Integration Points
- Session/connection builders
- Query execution methods
- Error handling paths
- Schema operations

## Agent Safety Preserved
The facade maintains agent safety guarantees:
- Default is auto-detect mode
- Plain mode always available
- No breaking changes to stdout semantics

## Dependencies
- Phase 2-7 complete (all console infrastructure ready)
- All driver integrations complete

## Verification
- Test prelude imports work
- Test builder integration
- Test global console pattern
- Verify feature flags work correctly","acceptance_criteria":"sqlmodel prelude re-exports console types when feature enabled
Session and Connection builders accept console configuration
Global console support works for convenience usage
Feature-gated compilation works correctly
All unit tests pass for facade integration
Documentation shows correct usage patterns","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:14:06.150467154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:45:52.471189789Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:45:52.471146628Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-318","depends_on_id":"bd-88i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:18:33.713314577Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-318","depends_on_id":"bd-eqb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:14:06.169155410Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":25,"issue_id":"bd-318","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Console types exported in sqlmodel prelude
- [ ] Session::builder().with_console() works
- [ ] Global console via sqlmodel::set_global_console()
- [ ] Console propagates from session to connection
- [ ] Feature flag 'console' properly gates all functionality
- [ ] No compile errors without console feature
- [ ] Integration tests verify API surface","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:34Z"},{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-318","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed thread-safety issue in global console by removing the rich_rust::Console field from SqlModelConsole (which contained Cell/RefCell types that are not Sync). The rich console functionality is still available but will be created on-demand rather than stored. All tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:45:36Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-31l","title":"Implement Session.get() for primary key lookup","description":"## Description

Get object by primary key, checking identity map first.

## Python Behavior

```python
user = session.get(User, 1)  # Get User with id=1
user = session.get(User, (1, 2))  # Composite PK

# With options:
user = session.get(User, 1, with_for_update=True)
user = session.get(User, 1, options=[joinedload(User.addresses)])
```

Key behavior:
1. Check identity map first (return cached if exists)
2. Query database if not in identity map
3. Return None if not found

## Rust Implementation

```rust
impl Session {
    pub async fn get<M: Model>(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        pk: impl Into<PrimaryKey>,
    ) -> Outcome<Option<M>, Error> {
        // 1. Check identity map
        if let Some(cached) = self.identity_map.get::<M>(&pk) {
            return Ok(Some(cached.clone()));
        }
        
        // 2. Query database
        let result = select!(M)
            .filter(M::primary_key().eq(pk))
            .first(cx, &self.conn)
            .await?;
        
        // 3. Add to identity map if found
        if let Some(ref model) = result {
            self.identity_map.insert(model);
        }
        
        Ok(result)
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Returns from identity map if present
- [ ] Queries DB if not in identity map
- [ ] Adds to identity map after DB fetch
- [ ] Returns None if not found
- [ ] Works with composite primary keys
- [ ] with_for_update option supported","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:05:03.803192332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:14:32.954426610Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31l","depends_on_id":"bd-2pkb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31l","depends_on_id":"bd-emz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-31l","title":"Implement Session.get() for primary key lookup","description":"## Description

Get object by primary key, checking identity map first.

## Python Behavior

```python
user = session.get(User, 1)  # Get User with id=1
user = session.get(User, (1, 2))  # Composite PK

# With options:
user = session.get(User, 1, with_for_update=True)
user = session.get(User, 1, options=[joinedload(User.addresses)])
```

Key behavior:
1. Check identity map first (return cached if exists)
2. Query database if not in identity map
3. Return None if not found

## Rust Implementation

```rust
impl Session {
    pub async fn get<M: Model>(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        pk: impl Into<PrimaryKey>,
    ) -> Outcome<Option<M>, Error> {
        // 1. Check identity map
        if let Some(cached) = self.identity_map.get::<M>(&pk) {
            return Ok(Some(cached.clone()));
        }
        
        // 2. Query database
        let result = select!(M)
            .filter(M::primary_key().eq(pk))
            .first(cx, &self.conn)
            .await?;
        
        // 3. Add to identity map if found
        if let Some(ref model) = result {
            self.identity_map.insert(model);
        }
        
        Ok(result)
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Returns from identity map if present
- [ ] Queries DB if not in identity map
- [ ] Adds to identity map after DB fetch
- [ ] Returns None if not found
- [ ] Works with composite primary keys
- [ ] with_for_update option supported","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:05:03.803192332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:09:51.737374394Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:09:51.736912393Z","close_reason":"Added get_by_pk, get_with_options, and GetOptions with FOR UPDATE support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31l","depends_on_id":"bd-2pkb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-31l","depends_on_id":"bd-emz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-33gi","title":"Implement read replicas support","description":"## Description

Route read queries to replicas, writes to primary.

## Python Behavior

```python
from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

write_engine = create_engine('postgresql://primary/db')
read_engine = create_engine('postgresql://replica/db')

class RoutingSession(Session):
    def get_bind(self, mapper=None, clause=None):
        if self._flushing or self.is_active:
            return write_engine
        return read_engine
```

## Rust Implementation

```rust
pub struct ReplicaPool {
    primary: Pool,
    replicas: Vec<Pool>,
    strategy: ReplicaStrategy,
}

pub enum ReplicaStrategy {
    RoundRobin,
    Random,
    LeastConnections,
}

impl ReplicaPool {
    pub async fn get_read(&self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<PooledConnection, Error> {
        // Get from replica
    }
    
    pub async fn get_write(&self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<PooledConnection, Error> {
        // Always primary
    }
}

// Session integration
impl Session {
    pub fn route_to_replica(&mut self, enable: bool) {
        self.use_replica = enable;
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ReplicaPool with primary + replicas
- [ ] Automatic read/write routing
- [ ] Multiple replica strategies
- [ ] Failover on replica failure
- [ ] Lag-aware routing (optional)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:12:15.844112835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:12:15.857870503Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33gi","depends_on_id":"bd-u73","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-34u","title":"Fix MySQL Connection trait - implement SharedMySqlConnection with interior mutability","description":"The MySqlAsyncConnection implements Connection trait but all methods return error stubs like 'Query requires mutable access' because the trait requires &self but MySQL operations need &mut self.

The fix is to implement SharedMySqlConnection (like PostgreSQL's SharedPgConnection) that wraps the connection in Arc<Mutex<_>> for interior mutability.

Reference: PostgreSQL implementation at crates/sqlmodel-postgres/src/async_connection.rs SharedPgConnection
Issue location: crates/sqlmodel-mysql/src/async_connection.rs lines 1552-1693","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:29:09.114453485Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:29:25.663522586Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:29:25.663456063Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - SharedMySqlConnection uses Arc<Mutex<_>> and properly implements Connection trait (lines 1998-2018)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
